title: Poor performance in large concat tree
body:
I encountered poor performance when using Verilator to convert assignments between large bit width signals. This is the example below. In the generated code, a large number of temporary variables, "__Vtemp_+<number>", will be used (the number will increase from 249 to 1913, and the bit width will increase from 2048 to 15360), and the VL_CONCAT_WIW function will be used extensively (approximately 1665 times) to continuously perform memcpy and concatenation from __ Vtemp_249, all the way to __Vtemp_1913, and finally to the final __Vcellout_ shift_exceed_ch_sig_merge. The intermediate memcpys are too time-consuming, resulting in poor execution performance of the entire program.

# example
```verilog
// shift_exceed.v
module shift_exceed
#(
    parameter CH_NUM = 640,
    parameter CN_W = 22
)(
    input [CH_NUM-1:0] pd_fp_ind,
    input [CH_NUM-1:0] ch_en,
    input [CH_NUM * CN_W-1:0] ch_in,
    output reg [CH_NUM * (CN_W+2) - 1:0] ch_sig_merge
);

genvar i1;
generate
for (i1 = 0; i1 < CH_NUM; i1=i1+1) begin: GEN_SIG_MERGE
    assign ch_sig_merge[i1 * (CN_W+2) +: (CN_W+2) ] = {
            pd_fp_ind[i1],
            ch_en[i1],
            ch_in[i1 * CN_W +: CN_W]
        };
end 
endgenerate

endmodule
```
# command line
`verilator --sc --debug --gdbbt --Wno-fatal ../shift_exceed.v`

# verilator --version
Verilator 5.024 2024-04-05 rev UNKNOWN.REV
I have already tried it with the git master version. The problem still exists.

#  OS distribution 
CentOS Linux release 7.9.2009 (Core)

# May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?
Yes, please. I haven't found the root cause yet, I don't know how to fix it.
